column3: Lookout Road at A37 (Suburb:New Lambton, Region:Hunter)
column14: Allow extra travel time; Plan your journey
column15: Changed traffic conditions will be in place for up to four consecutive weekends from Friday 28 March, between 7pm Friday and 5am Monday, weather permitting. The work is part of the construction the Southern Interchange of the Rankin Park to Jesmond section of the Newcastle Inner City Bypass. Changed traffic conditions include: Temporary traffic lane realignment and modification of the intersection including closure of slip lanes on McCaffrey Drive and Lookout Road, as well as the closure of one right turn lane from Lookout Road into McCaffrey DriveLookout Road will be reduced to one lane both northbound and southbound each weekendMcCaffrey Drive will be closed in both directions between Marshall Street and Lookout Road on the third weekend of work between 7pm Friday and 5am Monday, weather permitting.
column16: Diversion for McCaffrey Drive closure is up to five kilometres via Grandview Road, Croudace Road, Cardiff Road, Wallsend Road, Main Road, Cardiff Road, Lookout Road will be in place during this time, which may add up to 15 minutes of travel time to journeys.

column19: Title: Advice for operators of OSOM vehicles,Details: Heavy vehicles with a ground contact or with overall width greater than 3m are advised to contact the project team at least one day ahead of movement on 1800 818 433. Significant delays will occur if contact is not made in time.Freight operators are advised the project operates on UHF channel 52xt the Southern Interchange. Vehicles travelling under a permit must not travel off the approved route listed in the permit unless an updated permit is obtained from the National Heavy Vehicle Regulator. ,

column3: East Seaham Road at  (Suburb:East Seaham, Region:Hunter)
column15: The scope of the project includes:Reconstructing the entire 3.1km unsealed section  to become sealed and match road widths of previously constructed stages.Upgrading drainage infrastructure to cater for large storm events and flooding.Installation of road safety barriers where required.OSOM InformationEast Seaham Road will be closed in both directions for upgrade works until early 2026.This closure will affect existing heavy vehicle detours in place due to current load limits on Brig O’Johnston Bridge at Clarence Town. Articulated trucks, vehicles wider than 2.8 metres and vehicles heavier than 22.5 tonnes are advised to use the Pacific Highway and Italia Road instead and allow extra travel time.
column16: Access options depend on where your property is located as below:East Seaham Rd, East Seaham (Port Stephens Council resident) - resident, emergency services, waste services and postal services access will only be available from the south (Italia Rd). Arrangements have been made for school bus services.East Seaham Rd, Clarence Town (Dungog Shire Council resident) - resident, emergency services, waste services and postal services access will only be available from the north (Limeburners Creek Rd). Arrangements have been made for school bus services.

column14: Allow extra travel time; Exercise caution
column15: Motorists are advised of changed overnight traffic conditions for work under the New England Highway. Off-ramp and lane closures will be in place while work is carried out in stages with detours in place.
column16: Stage 1: Motorists traveling north toward Thornton will not be able to make a right turn from the Weakleys Drive off-ramp. Motorist will need to turn left onto Weakleys Drive and use the roundabout to continue to travel northbound onto Weakleys Drive.  Stage 2: Motorists traveling west on the New England Highway will not be able to exit at the Weakleys Drive off-ramp. Motorist will need to continue on the New England Highway to the u-turn bay and use the eastbound exit to turn back onto Weakleys Drive.  Stage 3: There will be no right turn for motorists travelling south from Weakley Drive off-ramp. Motorists will need to turn left onto Weakleys Drive and use the roundabout to continue onto Weakleys Drive.
